title: Smoky Flaky Russian Salmon Skillet
description: A high‑protein, smoky salmon skillet featuring tomato puree and fresh herbs for a quick, solo‑dinner Russian‑inspired meal.
instructions: Season the salmon fillets on both sides with smoked paprika, smoked salt, and black pepper.
Heat the skillet over medium‑high and add the olive oil. Sear the salmon fillets skin side down for about 3‑4 minutes until the skin is crisp.
Flip the fillets, add the butter, minced garlic, diced onion, and let everything sizzle for 1 minute to meld the flavors.
Pour in the tomato puree, then stir in lemon zest, dill, and parsley. Bring to a gentle simmer for 2 minutes.
Transfer the skillet (still covered if it’s oven‑safe) to the preheated oven and bake for 8‑10 minutes, or until the salmon flakes easily and reaches an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C).
Remove the skillet from the oven, let the salmon rest for 2 minutes, then slide the fillets onto plates.
Serve immediately, optionally garnishing with a sprinkle of fresh dill or parsley.
